meta.description: Night-shift work queue for TheMaximoGuys blog. One item is processed per cron tick (hourly, 23:00-06:00). Source of truth for the backlog: content-planning/DOCS-TO-BLOGS-GAP-ANALYSIS.md
meta.style_rotation_note: Cover styles rotate per series: SketchNote (sketched pager, primary), DanKoeStyle (frameworks/mental models), InfoBlocks (3D isometric), BlueprintBoard (engineering/architecture topics only). Never raw single-prompt generation, never Flash tier.

items.topic: DEEPEN existing draft: Dispatching & the Phased Rollout
items.brief: EXPANSION JOB, explicitly permitted to edit this existing draft:true post: posts/MAS-OPTIMIZER/2026-07-16-mas-optimizer-05-dispatching-rollout.mdx (3667 words). Apply the full deep-research protocol (DOC2 §8 re-read + SearchMaximo sweep + 3 web verifications) and expand to 4200+ words with researched substance: more dispatcher scenarios, re-optimization edge cases, troubleshooting section, additional tables. Keep the existing cover; do NOT regenerate it. Keep draft:true.

items.topic: DEEPEN existing draft: Extension Crossovers
items.brief: EXPANSION JOB, explicitly permitted to edit this existing draft:true post: posts/MAS-JAVA-EXTENSIONS/2026-07-16-mas-java-extensions-07-extension-crossovers.mdx (3601 words). Apply the full deep-research protocol (DOC6 §8.5 re-read + SearchMaximo sweep + 3 web verifications) and expand to 4300+ words (series upper range) with researched substance: deeper Nuclear clone upgrade guidance, more MAXOBJECT diagnostics, a troubleshooting section, Type 5 worked examples. Keep the existing cover; do NOT regenerate it. Keep draft:true.

items.topic: Dispatching and Rollout: Putting the Optimizer into Daily Operations
items.brief: The index already links a Part 5 that does not exist. Cover optimizer schedule dispatching back to Manage work orders, crew acceptance workflows, phased rollout, and measuring schedule quality. Fix index navigation chain to include this part.

items.topic: MAS + Databricks Lakehouse: Series Index
items.brief: Series index for a 6-part MAS-to-Databricks lakehouse analytics series (source: DOC5). Written LAST conceptually but can be drafted first as an outline index and updated as parts land; follow the pattern of posts/MAS-NUCLEAR/*00-series-index.mdx.

items.topic: Why Your Maximo Data Belongs in a Lakehouse
items.brief: The case for Databricks alongside MAS native analytics: limits of Cognos/BIRT/Monitor dashboards, cross-domain analytics, data gravity, cost of copies. Position vs MAS Health/Predict native scoring.

items.topic: Getting Maximo Data Out: Kafka, Data Export Framework, and CDC Patterns
items.brief: Architecture patterns for MAS-to-Databricks pipelines: MAS data export framework, Kafka/event streams, JDBC batch, object structures via REST, CDC considerations, landing into Delta Lake bronze.

items.topic: Building the Asset Lakehouse: Bronze, Silver, Gold for Maximo Objects
items.brief: Medallion architecture applied to core Maximo objects (WORKORDER, ASSET, MATUSETRANS, PM, meter data): cleansing rules, conformed dimensions, gold marts for reliability and cost analytics.

items.topic: Five Analytics Use Cases: Reliability, Cost, Inventory, Backlog, Compliance
items.brief: Concrete SQL/dashboard-level use cases on the gold layer: MTBF/MTTR trends, maintenance cost rollups, slow-moving inventory, backlog aging, PM compliance. Tie back to MAS-RELIABILITY series metrics.

items.topic: Custom ML in Databricks vs MAS Predict: When to Use Which
items.brief: Decision framework comparing MAS Predict native models against custom MLflow models in Databricks: data needs, skills, AppPoints economics, writing scores back into MAS Health.

items.topic: Governance and Security for the MAS Lakehouse: Unity Catalog, Access, Lineage
items.brief: Unity Catalog governance over Maximo-derived data, row/column security mirroring Maximo site/org security, lineage, PII in labor records, audit expectations.

items.topic: AI Parts Identifier: Series Index
items.brief: Index for 5-part computer-vision parts identification series (source: DOC2). Directory posts/MAS-PARTS-IDENTIFIER exists but is empty. Cross-link to MAS-SUPPLY-CHAIN storeroom posts and MAS-FEATURES parts 14/24.

items.topic: What Is the AI Parts Identifier and Why Storerooms Need It
items.brief: Computer-vision part recognition in MAS: the unknown-part problem at the storeroom counter, time wasted on manual catalog lookups, where Parts Identifier sits in the MAS suite.

items.topic: Setting Up and Training the Parts Model
items.brief: Image dataset preparation, item master hygiene prerequisites, training/validation workflow, retraining cadence, accuracy expectations.

items.topic: Storeroom Workflows: Snap, Identify, Issue
items.brief: End-to-end storeroom flow: photo capture, candidate matches, confirming the item, issuing against a work order. Tie into MAS-SUPPLY-CHAIN storekeeper posts.

items.topic: Parts Identifier in MAS Mobile: Field Technician Scenarios
items.brief: Mobile capture in the field, offline behavior, technician identifying a failed component at the asset, requesting the part from the truck or storeroom.

items.topic: Accuracy, Governance, and AppPoints for Parts Identifier
items.brief: Measuring match accuracy, feedback loops, model governance, AppPoints/licensing consumption, rollout guardrails.

items.topic: Maximo for Civil Infrastructure: Series Index
items.brief: Index for 5-part civil infrastructure series (bridges, roads, DOT audience). Source DOC2.

items.topic: Maximo for Civil Infrastructure: Bridges, Roads, and Public Assets
items.brief: What the Civil Infrastructure add-on covers, who it is for (DOT, municipalities), how it extends core Manage.

items.topic: Linear Assets Fundamentals: Measures, Segments, and Features
items.brief: Linear asset modeling for roads/rail/pipelines: linear referencing, measures, segmentation, features and relationships.

items.topic: Inspections and Condition Assessment for Civil Assets
items.brief: Inspection forms, condition ratings, NBI-style bridge inspection cycles, feeding condition into asset health.

items.topic: From Condition to Capital Plan: Prioritizing Infrastructure Investment
items.brief: Using condition and criticality data for capital planning, deterioration curves, budget scenario prioritization.

items.topic: Regulatory Reporting and Compliance for Public Infrastructure
items.brief: FHWA/NBI reporting, audit trails, public accountability dashboards, records retention.

items.topic: Reporting Options in MAS 9: Cognos, BIRT, Dashboards, and Beyond
items.brief: Standalone post (DOC1 gap): the reporting landscape in MAS 9 and how to choose. Save at posts/ root with date prefix like other standalone posts.

items.topic: MAS 9 Upgrade Gotchas: What Actually Breaks and How to Prepare
items.brief: Standalone post (DOC1 gap): real-world upgrade pitfalls — customizations, integrations, Java version, admin mode windows.

items.topic: Java Extension Crossovers: Where Automation Scripts End and Java Begins
items.brief: Standalone post (DOC6 gap): decision framework for automation scripts vs Java extensions, migration paths, hybrid patterns. Cross-link MAS-JAVA-EXTENSIONS series.
items.note: Superseded 2026-07-16: written interactively as series Part 7 (mas-java-extensions-extension-crossovers) with correct DOC6 §8.5 scope

items.topic: MAS 9 + watsonx.data: Building the Maximo Open Lakehouse (Series Index)
items.brief: Series index for a 6-part MAS-to-watsonx.data open-lakehouse series (source: knowledge_base/DOC13). IBM-native counterpart to the MAS-DATABRICKS series. Written as an outline index following posts/MAS-DATABRICKS/*00-series-index.mdx and posts/MAS-NUCLEAR/*00-series-index.mdx pattern; link all parts, cross-reference the Databricks series as the non-IBM alternative.

items.topic: Why watsonx.data: IBM's Open Lakehouse Answer for Maximo
items.brief: Source: DOC13 Part 2 + Part 1. The IBM-native case: where MAS native analytics (Health/Predict/Monitor/Cognos/AI Service) stops, and why watsonx.data (open Apache Iceberg, fit-for-purpose engines, one-vendor governance, watsonx.ai already bridged via MAS AI Service) fills the gap. Honest framing: this is the IBM path; Databricks (DOC5 series) is the non-IBM alternative for the same gap. Iceberg vs Delta, open vs runtime-coupled.

items.topic: Getting Maximo Data into watsonx.data: MIF, Kafka, and Bulk Export
items.brief: Source: DOC13 Part 3 (5.1-5.2). Three IBM-documented extraction patterns: MIF REST/JSON (OSLC, synonym-domain internal values, API-key auth), Kafka source connector (near-real-time), MAS 9.1 async bulk export to S3/MIF global directory. Cloud Pak for Data as the fabric; watsonx.data Presto connection asset (port 443, engine 8443); Db2 Warehouse operator for standalone Manage. Core tables WORKORDER/ASSET/FAILUREREPORT/MEASUREMENT with named columns. Note SaaS-vs-self-managed DB-direct constraint.

items.topic: The Iceberg Medallion: Bronze, Silver, Gold for Maximo Objects on watsonx.data
items.brief: Source: DOC13 Part 3 (5.3-5.4) + Part 2 (4.3). Medallion on Apache Iceberg: Bronze raw Maximo extracts, Silver conformed EAM entities (ASSET_DIM, WORKORDER_FACT, FAILURE_FACT, MEASUREMENT_FACT, synonym-decoded), Gold feature/KPI tables. Iceberg specifics: ACID, schema evolution, time-travel snapshots, Db2 CREATE DATALAKE TABLE ... STORED BY ICEBERG syntax, bucket-level vs table-level registration, Sync metadata modes. Why Iceberg maps cleanly onto changing Maximo schemas.

items.topic: Fit-for-Purpose Engines: Presto, Spark, and Db2 over One Iceberg Copy
items.brief: Source: DOC13 Part 2 (4.2) + Part 5. IBM's multi-engine strategy over shared Iceberg tables: Presto (Java) interactive+federation (coordinator/worker/resource-manager), Presto C++/Velox (Presto 2.0, v0.286, IBM query optimizer, price/perf claims vs Databricks Photon), Spark for ETL/ML/table maintenance, Db2 Warehouse + Netezza for BI concurrency. When to route which EAM workload to which engine; the cost argument (RU=$1, per-second metering, 'up to 50% DW cost reduction').

items.topic: From Lakehouse to Action: watsonx.ai, Granite, RAG, and Closed-Loop Write-Back
items.brief: Source: DOC13 Part 2 (4.5-4.6), Part 3 (5.5), Part 4 + DOC14. watsonx.ai over the lakehouse: custom PdM/RUL models, AutoAI for RAG, Prompt Lab chat-with-docs; RAG stack Milvus + OpenSearch + OpenRAG (Docling/Langflow) over maintenance manuals + WO free-text; Granite/Llama foundation models. Closed-loop write-back three ways: AI Service-mediated, direct Maximo REST (POST /os/mxwo, PUT /os/mxitem), and watsonx Orchestrate (IBM/maximo-wxo-integration repo). Cross-reference the watsonx platform doc (DOC14).

items.topic: watsonx.data vs Databricks vs MAS Native: Choosing the IBM-Native Path (and Governing It)
items.brief: Source: DOC13 Part 5 + Part 2 (4.4). Head-to-head decision framework: Iceberg vs Delta, Presto C++/Velox vs Photon, IBM Knowledge Catalog vs Unity Catalog, Milvus/OpenRAG vs Databricks vector; honest pros/cons of each; when to pick watsonx.data vs Databricks vs keep MAS-native. Governance: IKC/Apache Ranger policy engine, mapping Maximo site/org security to row/column policies, LABTRANS PII masking, watsonx.governance lineage. Flag IBM benchmarks as internal, not independent.
